does: This paper announces the creation of an industry consortium dedicated to advancing safe frontier AI development through coordinated research, standard-setting, and governance. The forum establishes a collaborative mechanism where leading AI developers can jointly invest in safety research, identify and document best practices for responsible development, and create shared technical standards. It facilitates structured information exchange between industry participants and policymakers to ensure that governance frameworks are informed by technical realities while safety concerns drive development practices.
connects: This work builds on the growing recognition that frontier AI safety requires collective action beyond individual company efforts, extending the tradition of industry consortiums like Partnership on AI while responding to recent calls from researchers and policymakers for greater coordination on AI risks. It connects to parallel efforts in responsible AI frameworks and governance initiatives that have emerged as frontier models have grown more capable and their societal impacts more significant. The forum represents an institutional response to the challenge of scaling safety practices across the industry and ensuring that dispersed research efforts can contribute to shared understanding.
enables: This institutional structure enables several downstream developments: standardized evaluation methodologies and safety benchmarks that can be shared across organizations, coordinated red-teaming and adversarial testing of frontier systems, and centralized repositories of safety research findings that accelerate industry-wide learning. The forum creates infrastructure for translating safety research into engineering practices and allows policymakers to access technical expertise during regulatory development. It opens pathways for establishing common definitions of capability thresholds and risk levels, potentially enabling more effective regulatory oversight and reducing competitive pressure that might otherwise compromise safety investments.
